title: Add bee backlog propose: promote a work item to a PBI row with an auto-assigned ID
read_first: skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs handleBacklogAdd (L2556), handleBacklogCounts/handleBacklogRank/handleBacklogBadges (L2456-2489), HANDLERS dispatch table backlog.* entries (~L5165-5168)
skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs (the sync script invoked at the end of this cell)
action: Per D1/D2/D3/D5 (plan.md Approach): add proposePbiRow(root, { story, cos, feature }) to lib/backlog.mjs, reusing backlogPath/resolveProductRoot and the same row-parsing readBacklogCounts uses. Scan EVERY row (including status-parser-unrecognized ones like link-annotated done rows) for the highest existing P<n> id across the whole table, assign n+1 (per D2, the P58 gap is never backfilled -- confirm with a table fixture that has a gap; the live table's actual current max keeps advancing as more rows are filed, so never hardcode an expected next id in a test -- read the max back out of the fixture, not a literal like P83). Append one new row with Status=proposed (per D1/D3 -- this verb only ever writes proposed, never in-flight/done) and Feature defaulting to '—' when --feature is omitted (per plan.md PD2). Validate --story (required, <=200 chars, matching BACKLOG_MAX_TITLE's existing convention) and --cos (required, <=2000 chars, per plan.md PD1) BEFORE any write -- any rejection must leave docs/backlog.md untouched, matching handleBacklogAdd's 'any rejection leaves the file untouched' convention (plan.md Test matrix #7). Add a thin handleBacklogPropose wrapper in bee.mjs (mirror handleBacklogCounts's shape, not handleBacklogAdd's -- this targets docs/backlog.md, not .bee/backlog.jsonl) that calls proposePbiRow and returns the assigned id + a one-line confirmation text; register it in bee.mjs's HANDLERS dispatch table (~L5111, backlog.* entries ~L5165-5168) as 'backlog.propose'. Add a new backlog.propose entry to lib/command-registry.mjs (model on the existing backlog.add entry's parameters/examples shape at L1060: properties story/cos/feature/json, required: [story, cos]). Add test coverage to test_cli_cells.mjs (existing backlog-add block starts ~L588): (a) a fresh table assigns P1 when no P<n> rows exist; (b) a table with an existing gap (e.g. a missing P<n> mid-sequence) still assigns max+1, not the gap; (c) --story/--cos over their length caps is rejected and the file is left byte-unchanged; (d) the new row is immediately readable by readBacklogCounts as a proposed row (counts.proposed increments by 1). After the template edit, run the exact sync script skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply so .bee/bin/bee.mjs, .bee/bin/lib/backlog.mjs, and .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs mirror the change byte-for-byte -- confirm all three copies match post-sync (this repo tracks 6 mirror roots total per onboard_bee.mjs's own managed-target list; the sync command updates all of them in one run).

truths: bee backlog propose --story '<s>' --cos '<c>' appends a new | P<n+1> | <s> | <c> | proposed | — | row to docs/backlog.md and returns the assigned id
the assigned id is always (highest existing P<n> across the whole table) + 1, even when the table has a gap (e.g. the existing P58 gap) or an unusual/unrecognized-status row
a --story or --cos over its length cap, or either flag missing, is rejected before any write -- docs/backlog.md is byte-identical to before the call
the new row is immediately counted by readBacklogCounts as proposed
.bee/bin/bee.mjs, .bee/bin/lib/backlog.mjs, and .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs match skills/bee-hive/templates/ byte-for-byte after sync

artifacts.substantive: exports proposePbiRow; scans the full table for the max existing P<n> id, appends a validated proposed row, never backfills gaps
handleBacklogPropose registered under 'backlog.propose' in HANDLERS, validates flags before calling proposePbiRow
backlog.propose entry documents story/cos/feature/json parameters and a runnable example

key_links: bee.mjs's handleBacklogPropose imports and calls lib/backlog.mjs's proposePbiRow
command-registry.mjs's backlog.propose example is runnable against the real dispatcher
prohibitions: Never write a Status other than 'proposed' for a row this verb creates (D1/D3)
Never auto-invoke bee-qualifying/bee-exploring or any other skill from this verb (D3)
Never read or import .bee/backlog.jsonl proposal entries (D4)
Never backfill the P58 gap or any other missing id (D2)
Never refactor bee-exploring's D11a or bee-scribing's D8 prose/logic in this cell (D5 -- filed separately as backlog row P82)

trace.outcome: Added bee backlog propose: proposePbiRow (lib/backlog.mjs) scans the whole table for the highest existing P<n> id and appends a new proposed row (max+1, gap never backfilled); handleBacklogPropose (bee.mjs) validates --story (<=200 chars)/--cos (<=2000 chars) before any write and defaults --feature to '—'; registered as backlog.propose in command-registry.mjs and HANDLERS; 8 new tests in test_cli_cells.mjs plus one registry-example-coverage test in test_bee_cli.mjs (deviation, see below); mirrors synced and byte-verified across .bee/bin, .claude-plugin, .codex-plugin, .claude, .agents; full run_verify.mjs green twice in a row (88/88 suites).
deviations: Touched skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs (not in the cell's declared files list) to add a registry-example-coverage test for the new backlog.propose entry — the full run_verify.mjs chain's existing 'every registry entry had its example executed at least once' guard failed once command-registry.mjs gained the entry, so this was a blocking fix (rule 3) required for the cell's own recorded verify command to pass, not a scope expansion.

Ran skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --apply, scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs, and scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write (all pre-existing repo mechanics, none new) to settle and re-hash the .bee/bin/**, .claude-plugin/**, .codex-plugin/**, .claude/**, .agents/** mirrors after each template edit, since the cell's own action step named the onboard_bee.mjs sync and the full verify chain also checks the release manifest and plugin-tree hashes.

checks.evidence: proposePbiRow (skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/backlog.mjs:89-101) scans every '|' line matching /^P(\d+)$/i on cells[0] with no status filter (max across whole table incl. unrecognized rows), writes '| P<n> | story | cos | proposed | feature |' with feature defaulting to '—'; handleBacklogPropose (bee.mjs) validates required+length before the sole write; gap/reject/counts tests in test_cli_cells.mjs confirm max+1-not-gap, byte-untouched-on-reject, and counts.proposed++
diff -q of skills/bee-hive/templates/{bee.mjs,lib/backlog.mjs,lib/command-registry.mjs} vs .bee/bin equivalents all printed MATCH

D1 verb+flags registered as backlog.propose in HANDLERS (bee.mjs) and command-registry.mjs; D2 max+1 no backfill (scan+gap test); D3 handler returns after write with no skill invocation; D4 proposePbiRow reads only backlogPath (docs/backlog.md), never .bee/backlog.jsonl; D5 new lib fn + registry entry mirrored, and diff touches no bee-exploring/bee-scribing prose

undeclared test_bee_cli.mjs adds a backlog.propose example-coverage test; the 'every registry entry had its example executed at least once' guard pre-exists at 88b3141^:skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs:2679, so the new registry entry tripped it — a required blocking-fix, not scope creep
